chapter_title.ar: باب فَضْلِ الْمَسْجِدِ الْأَعْظَمِ بِالْكُوفَةِ وَ فَضْلِ الصَّلَاةِ فِيهِ وَ الْمَوَاضِعِ الْمَحْبُوبَةِ فِيهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 102 – The merit of the Grand Masjid of Al-Kufa, and the merit of the Salāt performed therein, and the beloved places in it

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/490)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: صحيح - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (15/490)
text: قَالَ مُعَاوِيَةُ بْنُ وَهْبٍ وَ أَخَذَ بِيَدِي وَ قَالَ قَالَ لِي أَبُو حَمْزَةَ وَ أَخَذَ بِيَدِي قَالَ وَ قَالَ لِي الْأَصْبَغُ بْنُ نُبَاتَةَ وَ أَخَذَ بِيَدِي فَأَرَانِي الْأُسْطُوَانَةَ السَّابِعَةَ فَقَالَ هَذَا مَقَامُ أَمِيرِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( صلوات الله عليه ) قَالَ وَ كَانَ الْحَسَنُ بْنُ عَلِيٍّ ( عليه السلام ) يُصَلِّي عِنْدَ الْخَامِسَةِ فَإِذَا غَابَ أَمِيرُ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ( عليه السلام ) صَلَّى فِيهَا الْحَسَنُ ( عليه السلام ) وَ هِيَ مِنْ بَابِ كِنْدَةَ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahya, from Muhammad Bin Al-Husayn, from Muhammad Bin Ismail Bin Bazie, from Abu Ismai Al-Sarraj who said,

‘Muawiya bin Wahab grabbed my hand and said, ‘Abu Hamza said to me, and he grabbed my hand, and said, ‘Al-Asbagh Bin Nubata grabbed my hand and showed me the seventh pillar, so he said, ‘This is a place of Amir Al-Momineenasws, and Al-Husayn Bin Aliasws used to pray Salāt by the fifth. So when Amir Al-Momineenasws was absent, Al-Hassanasws prayed Salāt therein, and it if from the door of Kinda’.
en.sarwar: 8. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn al-Husayn from Muhammad ibn Isma’il ibn Bazi‘a from abu Isma’il al- Sarraj who has said the following: “Mu‘awiyah ibn Wahab, holding my hand, said to me, ‘Abu Hamzah holding my hand said to me, “Asbagh ibn Nubatah holding my hand showed me the seventh pillar and said, ‘This is the station of ’Amir al-Mu’minin, ‘Alayhi al-Salam He (the narrator) has said, ‘Al-Hassan ibn Ali, ‘Alayhi al- Salam, would perform Salah (prayer) near the fifth pillar. When ’Amir al-Mu’minin passed away, al- Hassan, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, performed Salah (prayer) at that place and it is from the side of Kindah door.’”
